2606.09578.title: TABVERSE: Benchmarking Cross-Format Table Understanding in LLMs and VLMs
2606.09578.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly evaluated on table reasoning tasks, but the role of table representation remains under-explored. In practice, the same table content may appear in different structural formats, such as HTML, Markdown, and LaTeX, or as rendered images. However, existing evaluations often let content, format, layout, and modality vary together, making it difficult to isolate representation effects. We introduce TABVERSE, a controlled multimodal table benchmark that aligns the same table content across multiple structural formats and rendered images, with question category and difficulty tags. This design enables systematic evaluation of representation effects while holding table content fixed. We evaluate LLMs and VLMs across three tasks: Question Answering (QA), Structural Understanding Capability (SUC), and Structure Reconstruction (SR). Our results show that representation choice substantially affects table understanding. Models generally perform better with structured text than with rendered images, but the size of this gap depends on the task, model, and format. HTML is often the most robust text format, while row-sensitive structural tasks and syntactically usable LaTeX reconstruction remain challenging. These findings show that table representation is a key factor in reliable table evaluation.
2606.09578.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）和視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在表格推理任務中的評估越來越多，但表格表示的角色仍然未被充分探索。<lb/>在實踐中，相同的表格內容可能以不同的結構格式出現，例如 HTML、Markdown 和 LaTeX，或作為渲染圖像。<lb/>然而，現有的評估通常讓內容、格式、佈局和模態一起變化，使得難以隔離表示效果。<lb/>我們介紹了 TABVERSE，一個受控的多模態表格基準，該基準在多個結構格式和渲染圖像中對齊相同的表格內容，並附有問題類別和難度標籤。<lb/>這種設計使得在固定表格內容的情況下，能夠系統地評估表示效果。<lb/>我們在三個任務上評估 LLMs 和 VLMs：問題回答（QA）、結構理解能力（SUC）和結構重建（SR）。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，表示選擇對表格理解有顯著影響。<lb/>模型通常在結構化文本上表現得比在渲染圖像上更好，但這一差距的大小取決於任務、模型和格式。<lb/>HTML 通常是最穩健的文本格式，而對行敏感的結構任務和語法可用的 LaTeX 重建仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/>這些發現顯示，表格表示是可靠表格評估中的一個關鍵因素。
2606.09578.author: Momina Ahsan et.al.
2606.09578.authors: Momina Ahsan, Sarfraz Ahmad, Ming Shan Hee, Roy Ka-Wei Lee, Preslav Nakov
